messages.content: 请帮我翻译，使其符合SCI写作标准
请提供您需要翻译的文本，以便我能够帮助您按照SCI（科学引文索引）期刊的写作标准来进行翻译。如果您有具体的文本内容，请直接提供；如果您有特定的要求或指导方针，请一并说明。

低红外发射率超黑材料是空间探测的关键材料。空间望远镜为避免杂散光干扰，其光学探测器表面会进行发黑处理，这导致探测器表面对巡天焦面漏热。为解决这一问题，中国科学院长春光学精密机械与物理研究所提出明确需求：研制低红外发射率的超黑材料，满足对可见和红外波段光的选择性吸收。针对上述需求，本项目提出一体式结构的低红外发射率超黑材料，通过实验结合理论模拟，开展红外发射率超黑材料的选择性吸收及机理研究。项目将基于离子径迹技术制备低红外发射率超黑材料，确定结构参数的调控方法，研究结构参数对选择性吸收特性的影响，揭示选择性吸收的机理。项目研究成果将为低红外发射率超黑材料在空间望远镜探测领域的应用提供关键实验数据和理论依据。

Ultra-black materials with low infrared emissivity are crucial for space exploration. To avoid stray light interference, optical detectors of space telescopes undergo blackening treatment, which results in thermal leakage on the detector surface from the celestial focal plane. To address this challenge, the Changchun Institute of Optics, Fine Mechanics and Physics at the Chinese Academy of Sciences has articulated a need for the development of ultra-black materials with low infrared emissivity that are capable of selective absorption of visible and infrared wavelengths.

In response to these needs, this project proposes an integrated structure of ultra-black materials with low infrared emissivity. Combining empirical experimentation with theoretical simulations, the project undertakes research into the selective absorption properties and underlying mechanisms of infrared emissivity in ultra-black materials. The project plans to leverage ion track technology to fabricate ultra-black materials with low infrared emissivity, ascertain methods to modulate structural parameters, investigate the impact of structural parameters on selective absorption characteristics, and unveil the mechanism behind selective absorption.

The research findings of the project will provide critical experimental data and theoretical foundation for the application of ultra-black materials with low infrared emissivity in the field of space telescope detection.

Ultra-black materials with low infrared emissivity are pivotal for space exploration to mitigate stray light in telescope detectors. The Changchun Institute of Optics, Fine Mechanics and Physics, Chinese Academy of Sciences, identifies the necessity for materials that selectively absorb visible and infrared light. This project addresses the demand by developing an integrated ultra-black material structure, merging practical experiments with theoretical simulations to explore the selective absorption mechanisms. Utilizing ion track technology, the project aims to produce ultra-black materials with tunable properties, elucidating the influence of structural parameters on absorption, thereby providing key data and theoretical support for space telescopes applications.
不够好，不要更改原文的结构和表达意思

Ultra-black materials with low infrared emissivity are key for space detection. To prevent stray light interference, space telescopes' optical detectors are blackened, causing thermal leakage on their surfaces from the sky survey focal plane. The Changchun Institute of Optics, Fine Mechanics and Physics at the Chinese Academy of Sciences demands the development of ultra-black materials with low infrared emissivity and selective absorption for visible and infrared light. This project proposes a monolithic structure for such materials and combines experiments with theoretical simulations to study their selective absorption and mechanisms. Utilizing ion track technology, the project aims to fabricate these materials, establish control methods for their structural parameters, investigate how these parameters affect selective absorption properties, and unveil the underlying absorption mechanisms. The outcomes will provide crucial experimental data and a theoretical basis for the application of low infrared emissivity ultra-black materials in space telescope detection.

为解决集成电路3D封装、倒封装等新型技术带来地面模拟单粒子效应困难的问题，HIRFL上高能单粒子效应研究平台应运而生。高能单粒子效应研究平台不仅仅是要提供足够的离子能量和射程来测试设备，更重要的是要提供单粒子效应的精确定位。本项目拟利用单片有源硅像素探测器位置精度高，抗辐射性能好等特点进行重离子击中位置探测，在此基础之上实现在线重离子击中位置定位，并结合单粒子效应事件检测系统，从而实现高能单粒子效应研究平台的在线单粒子效应精确定位系统。该系统的成功研制，有助于提升HIRFL高能单粒子效应研究平台的单粒子效应测试能力，并对国内抗辐射集成电路的发展做出贡献。

To address the challenges of simulating single-event effects (SEEs) on the ground for new technologies such as 3D integration and flip-chip packaging in integrated circuits, the high-energy single particle effects research platform at the Heavy Ion Research Facility in Lanzhou (HIRFL) was conceived. This platform aims not only to provide sufficient ion energy and range for device testing but, more importantly, precise localization of single-event effects.

This project intends to capitalize on the high positional accuracy and radiation resistance of monolithic active silicon pixel detectors to detect the impact locations of heavy ions. Building upon this, the project will accomplish online heavy ion impact localization, integrating it with the single-event effects event detection system. This will enable precise online localization of single-event effects on the high-energy single particle effects research platform at HIRFL. The successful development of this system will enhance HIRFL's capabilities in testing for single-event effects, and contribute to the development of radiation-hardened integrated circuits in China.
不够好，请重写，不要改变原文的意思

To overcome the difficulties of simulating single particle effects (SPE) on the ground due to emerging technologies like 3D integrated circuit packaging and flip-chipping, the High Energy Single Particle Effects Research Platform has emerged at HIRFL. This platform is designed to not just offer ample ion energy and range for testing devices, but more crucially, to provide precise localization of single particle effects. The project plans to utilize the high positional accuracy and excellent radiation resistance of the monolithic active silicon pixel detectors to detect the precise locations of heavy ion strikes. On this basis, it aims to achieve online localization of heavy ion strikes and, coupled with a SPE detection system, realize a precise online localization system for SPE studies on the platform. The successful development of this system is expected to significantly enhance the SPE testing capabilities at HIRFL and contribute to the advancement of radiation-hardened integrated circuits within China.

To tackle the increasing complexity in simulating single particle effects (SPEs) for novel integrated circuit technologies such as 3D packaging and flip-chip assemblies, the Heavy Ion Research Facility in Lanzhou (HIRFL) has developed a high-energy single particle effects research platform. This platform's primary objective is to ensure that sufficient ion energy and range are available for comprehensive device testing while delivering precise localization of SPEs. The project aims to leverage the high spatial accuracy and robust radiation tolerance of monolithic active silicon pixel detectors to identify heavy ion hit locations. In doing so, it will establish an online localization system for heavy ion impacts which, integrated with a SPE event detection system, will create an accurate online SPE localization capability for the HIRFL platform. The fruition of this system will bolster HIRFL’s testing proficiency for high-energy SPEs, contributing to the progression of radiation-tolerant integrated circuits in China.

为解决集成电路3D封装、倒封装等新型技术带来地面模拟单粒子效应困难的问题，HIRFL上高能单粒子效应研究平台应运而生。高能单粒子效应研究平台不仅仅是要提供足够的离子能量和射程来测试设备，更重要的是要提供单粒子效应的精确定位。本项目拟利用单片有源硅像素探测器位置精度高，抗辐射性能好等特点进行重离子击中位置探测，在此基础之上实现在线重离子击中位置定位，并结合单粒子效应事件检测系统，从而实现高能单粒子效应研究平台的在线单粒子效应精确定位系统。该系统的成功研制，有助于提升HIRFL高能单粒子效应研究平台的单粒子效应测试能力，并对国内抗辐射集成电路的发展做出贡献。

To remedy the challenges posed by terrestrial simulation of single particle effects (SPEs) in advanced integrated circuit technologies such as 3D packaging and flip-chip assemblies, the High Energy Single Particle Effects Research Platform at the Heavy Ion Research Facility in Lanzhou (HIRFL) was established. This platform is not only tasked with providing ample ion energy and range for comprehensive device testing, but its more critical function is to pinpoint the localization of SPEs with high precision. The current project proposes to exploit the high positional accuracy and superior radiation tolerance of monolithic active pixel silicon detectors for detecting the locations of heavy ion strikes. Building upon this capability, it aims to perform online localization of heavy ion impacts, which, in conjunction with the SPE detection system, will facilitate the development of an accurate online SPE localization system for the HIRFL platform. The successful development of this system promises to enhance the capacity of HIRFL for testing SPEs and contribute to the progress of radiation-hardened integrated circuits in China.

对不起造成的混乱。我将根据您的指示和之前的内容进行纠正，避免改变原意，并用SEE代替之前的SPE。

To address the difficulties of ground-based simulation of single event effects (SEEs) arising from new integrated circuit technologies such as 3D packaging and flip-chip assemblies, the High Energy Single Event Effects Research Platform at the Heavy Ion Research Facility in Lanzhou (HIRFL) has been developed. This platform aims to provide not only the necessary ion energy and range for comprehensive device testing, but more crucially, to achieve precise localization of SEEs. The specific objective of this project is to harness the high spatial resolution and excellent radiation resistance of monolithic active silicon pixel detectors for the detection of heavy ion strike locations. On this foundation, the project aims to realize an online heavy ion strike localization process, which, when integrated with a SEE event detection system, will implement an online SEE precise localization system on the HIRFL high-energy SEE research platform. Successful development of this system will enhance the SEE testing capability at HIRFL and contribute significantly to the field of radiation-hardened integrated circuits in China.
